rulings.Case Title: RICHARD LUCIA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 17, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff's objections 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6 to the Medeiros Declaration are sustained for lack of personal knowledge, foundation, and hearsay. Objection 3 is overruled. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action without opposition. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: DAIMLERCHRYSLER CORPORATION VS. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 17, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The issue presented is whether the lessor of a motor vehicle, who, pursuant to the Lemon Law, refunds to the lessee the lease payments and the use taxes paid, is entitled to a refund from SBE for the use tax collected and paid to SBE on behalf of the lessee. The Lemon Law, Civil Code section 1793.2, does not refer to leases but case law makes clear that this provision applies to leased vehicles. Jensen v. BMW of North America, Inc. (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 112; Brady v. Mercedes-Benz USA Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2002) 243 F.Supp.2d 1004. As a consequence the statute must be interpreted to be consistent with the treatment of sold vehicles. It is undisputed that sales taxes must be returned and the seller is entitled to a refund of sales taxes paid to SBE. There is no reason to treat use taxes in a different fashion, particularly since SBE agrees that the lessee would be entitled to a refund. The Court will address the parties evidentiary objections at the hearing as well as the statute of limitations issue. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 17, 2008, line 7. PLAINTIFF CHRYSLER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The issue presented is whether the lessor of a motor vehicle, who, pursuant to the Lemon Law, refunds to the lessee the lease payments and the use taxes paid, is entitled to a refund from SBE for the use tax collected and paid to SBE on behalf of the lessee. The Lemon Law, Civil Code section 1793.2, does not refer to leases but case law makes clear that this provision applies to leased vehicles. Jensen v. BMW of North America, Inc. (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 112; Brady v. Mercedes-Benz USA Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2002) 243 F.Supp.2d 1004. As a consequence the statute must be interpreted to be consistent with the treatment of sold vehicles. It is undisputed that sales taxes must be returned and the seller is entitled to a refund of sales taxes paid to SBE. There is no reason to treat use taxes in a different fashion, particularly since SBE agrees that the lessee would be entitled to a refund. The Court will address the parties evidentiary objections at the hearing as well as the statute of limitations issue. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY STEVENS VS. YOLANDA PAUL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees Incurred On Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 17, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT YOLANDA PAUL'S Motion For Attorney's Fees Incurred On Appeal is granted. Defendant is awarded $9387.50 in fees. The billing rate and hours expended are reasonable and defendant is the prevailing party. CCP section 1032 (a) (4); CRC 8.278 (a)(1) and (2); Moore v. Liu (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 745, 754-755. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ABOLFAZL ZADMEHR VS. JOSEPH BOYS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 17, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANTS JOSEPH BOYS, LLC, MOHSEN POURSOHI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 6th cause of action for plaintiff to clarify the terms of the written and oral contract alleged. Overruled as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th causes of action. An affirmative defense is not plead by the use of the word "manager" and the causes of action are not uncertain. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. UNITED COMMERCIAL BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Issuance Of A Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 17, 2008, line 14. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: ISSUANCE OF A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. The request for a preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that he is likely to prevail on the merits. The record reflects that plaintiff is an experienced real estate broker and the loan documents make clear that the interest is variable. Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that the loan was procured by fraud. =(302/PJM/PB)
